metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1825
metadata.comment:
Rationale: 7/6/2017 Pim
Fig.3: The extracellular domain of IL1RAPL1 interacts with that of PTPδ.
Fig.6: The IL1RAPL1/PTPδ complex recruits RhoGAP2 to synapses.

- PTPδ is a presynaptic protein, IL1RAPL1 is a postsynaptic protein, the complex formation can be seen as a handshaking between synaptic compartment and is considered as a 'signal'. The term 'trans-synaptic signaling by trans-synaptic complex (GO:0099545)' was applied.
- The role of PTPδ is not examined to the same extent as that of IL1RAPL1 int his paper. The application of the term 'trans-synaptic signaling by trans-synaptic complex (GO:0099545)' comes from the specificity of PTPδ as a presynaptic partner of IL1RAPL1 as shown in Fig.3.. Experimental description: Literal form M&M
"Cell culture, transfection, staining and quantification
of primary rat hippocampal neurons
Low-density hippocampal neuronal cultures were prepared from E18-E19 rat hippocampi as previously described with minor modifications"

7/6/2017 Pim
- Species of the over-expressed protein is traced back to **human** species via PMID:20139422. literal: "PTPδ-ecto-pDis and PTPσ-ecto-pDis have been previously described (19)"--> "...human PTP-δ-Ecto (NM_002839, aa 21-1174), ... "
